 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 51→ *
 52→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 53→ *
 54→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 55→ *
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 58→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 59→ *    ```
 60→ *
 61→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 62→ *
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 65→ *    var x: String
 66→ *    ```
 67→ *
 68→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 69→ *
 70→ *    ```
 71→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 72→ *    ```
 73→ *
 74→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 75→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 76→ *
 77→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 78→ */
 79→public class AnnotationRule :
 80→    StandardRule(
 81→        id = "annotation",
 82→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 83→            setOf(
 84→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 86→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 87→            ),
 88→        visitorModifiers =
 89→            setOf(
 90→                RunAfterRule(
 91→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 92→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 93→                ),
 94→            ),
 95→    ) {
 96→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 98→
 99→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
100→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
101→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
102→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
103→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
104→        )
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
114→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
115→            }
116→            ANNOTATION -> {
117→                // Annotation array
118→                //     @[...]
119→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
120→            }
121→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
122→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
123→            }
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
128→        node: ASTNode,
129→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
130→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
131→    ) {
132→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
133→
134→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPartOfPrimaryConstructorAnnotations()) {
135→            // When using ktlint_official code style, annotations on the primary constructor should be wrapped
136→            // to separate lines, same as other annotations
137→            checkForAnnotationOnPrimaryConstructorToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
138→            return
139→        }
140→
141→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
142→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
143→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
144→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
145→        ) {
146→            // Disallow:
147→            //     @Foo class Bar
148→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
149→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
150→            //     fun foo(
151→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
152→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
153→            //     ) {}
154→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
155→        }
156→
157→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
158→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
159→                // Code below is disallowed
160→                //   @Foo1
161→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
162→                emit(
163→                    node.startOffset,
164→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
165→                    true,
166→                )
167→                if (autoCorrect) {
168→                    node
169→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
170→                        .nextLeaf()
171→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
172→                }
173→            }
174→
175→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
176→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
177→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
178→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
179→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
180→            ) {
181→                // Code below is disallowed
182→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
183→                // But following is allowed:
184→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
185→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
186→                emit(
187→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
188→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
189→                    true,
190→                )
191→                if (autoCorrect) {
192→                    node
193→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
194→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
195→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
196→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
197→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
198→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
199→                                },
200→                        )
201→                }
202→            }
203→
204→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
205→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
206→                ?.let { startOfList ->
207→                    // Code below is disallowed
208→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
209→                    // But following is allowed:
210→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
211→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
212→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
216→                        if (autoCorrect) {
217→                            startOfList
218→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
219→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
220→                                )
221→                        }
222→                    }
223→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
224→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
225→                    ) {
226→                        node
227→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
228→                            .treeParent
229→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
230→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
231→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
232→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
233→                                if (autoCorrect) {
234→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
235→                                }
236→                            }
237→                    }
238→                }
239→        }
240→
241→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
242→            // Code below is disallowed
243→            //   @Foo1
244→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
245→            //   fun foo() {}
246→            emit(
247→                node.startOffset,
248→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
249→                true,
250→            )
251→            if (autoCorrect) {
252→                node
253→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
254→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
255→            }
256→        }
257→    }
258→
259→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
260→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
261→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
262→            ?.treeParent
263→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
264→            ?.treeParent
265→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
266→            ?.treeParent
267→
268→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfPrimaryConstructorAnnotations() =
269→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
270→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
271→            ?.treeParent
272→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR }
273→            .let { it != null }
274→
275→    private fun checkForAnnotationOnPrimaryConstructorToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
276→        node: ASTNode,
277→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
278→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
279→    ) {
280→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
281→        require(node.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST)
282→        require(node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
283→
284→        val modifierList = node.treeParent
285→
286→        // Check if this is the first annotation and needs a newline before it
287→        if (node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()) {
288→            val prevLeaf = modifierList.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }
289→            if (prevLeaf != null && !modifierList.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
290→                emit(
291→                    node.startOffset,
292→                    "Annotation on primary constructor should be placed on a separate line",
293→                    true,
294→                )
295→                if (autoCorrect) {
296→                    node
297→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
298→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructor(modifierList))
299→                }
300→            }
301→        }
302→
303→        // Check if annotation should be on separate line from other annotations on the same line
304→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
305→            emit(
306→                node.startOffset,
307→                "Annotation on primary constructor should be placed on a separate line",
308→                true,
309→            )
310→            if (autoCorrect) {
311→                node
312→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
313→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructor(modifierList))
314→            }
315→        }
316→
317→        // Check if this is the last annotation and needs a newline after it (before the constructor keyword)
318→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
319→            val constructorKeyword = modifierList
320→                .treeParent
321→                .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD)
322→            if (constructorKeyword != null) {
323→                val prevLeaf = constructorKeyword.prevLeaf()
324→                if (prevLeaf == null || !prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
325→                    emit(
326→                        constructorKeyword.startOffset,
327→                        "Expected newline before 'constructor'",
328→                        true,
329→                    )
330→                    if (autoCorrect) {
331→                        constructorKeyword.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructor(modifierList))
332→                    }
333→                }
334→            }
335→        }
336→    }
337→
338→    private fun getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructor(modifierList: ASTNode): String {
339→        // For primary constructor annotations, we need to find the indent of the class declaration
340→        // and add one level of indentation
341→        val primaryConstructor = modifierList.treeParent
342→        val classNode = primaryConstructor.treeParent
343→        val nodeBeforeClass = classNode.treePrev
344→        val indentWithoutNewline =
345→            nodeBeforeClass
346→                ?.text
347→                .orEmpty()
348→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
349→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline).plus(indentConfig.indent)
350→    }
351→
352→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
353→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
354→        return siblings(forward = false)
355→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
356→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
357→    }
358→
359→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
360→        node: ASTNode,
361→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
362→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
363→    ) {
364→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
365→            emit(
366→                node.startOffset,
367→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
368→                true,
369→            )
370→            if (autoCorrect) {
371→                node
372→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
373→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
374→            }
375→        }
376→
377→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
378→            emit(
379→                node.startOffset,
380→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
381→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
382→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
383→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
384→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
385→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
386→            )
387→            if (autoCorrect) {
388→                node
389→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
390→                    .nextLeaf()
391→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
392→                    ?.let {
393→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
394→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
395→                        } else {
396→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
397→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
398→                            )
399→                        }
400→                    }
401→            }
402→        }
403→    }
404→
405→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
406→
407→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
408→        psi
409→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
410→            ?.useSiteTarget
411→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
412→
413→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
414→
415→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
416→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
417→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
418→
419→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
420→        this ==
421→            treeParent
422→                .children()
423→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
424→
425→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
426→        this ==
427→            treeParent
428→                .children()
429→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
430→
431→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
432→        siblings(forward = false)
433→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
434→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
435→
436→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
437→        siblings()
438→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
439→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
440→
441→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
442→        siblings(forward = false)
443→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
444→
445→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
446→        siblings(forward = false)
447→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
448→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
449→
450→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
451→
452→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
453→        siblings(forward = true)
454→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
455→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
456→
457→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
458→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
459→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
460→            .leaves(forward = true)
461→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
462→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
463→    }
464→
465→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
466→        val astNode =
467→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
468→                treeParent
469→            } else {
470→                this
471→            }
472→        return checkNotNull(
473→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
474→        )
475→    }
476→
477→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
478→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
479→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
480→        } else {
481→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
482→        }
483→
484→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
485→        node: ASTNode,
486→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
487→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
488→    ) {
489→        node
490→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
491→            .nextCodeLeaf()
492→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
493→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
494→
495→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
496→                    emit(
497→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
498→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
499→                        true,
500→                    )
501→                    if (autoCorrect) {
502→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
503→                    }
504→                }
505→            }
506→    }
507→
508→    private fun visitAnnotation(
509→        node: ASTNode,
510→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
511→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
512→    ) {
513→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
514→
515→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
516→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
517→        ) {
518→            emit(
519→                node.startOffset,
520→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
521→                true,
522→            )
523→            if (autoCorrect) {
524→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
525→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
526→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
527→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
528→                }
529→            }
530→        }
531→    }
532→
533→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
534→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
535→            modifierListRoot
536→                .treeParent
537→                .treePrev
538→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
539→        val indentWithoutNewline =
540→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
541→                ?.text
542→                .orEmpty()
543→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
544→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
545→    }
546→}
547→
548→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
549→

## Summary of Changes

### 1. AnnotationRule.kt Changes:

1. **Added imports:**
   - `CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD`
   - `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR` 
   - `CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official`

2. **Added `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY` to `usesEditorConfigProperties`** to properly read the code style setting.

3. **Added new logic in `visitAnnotationEntry`:**
   - When using `ktlint_official` code style and the annotation is on a primary constructor, delegate to the new `checkForAnnotationOnPrimaryConstructorToBePlacedOnSeparateLine` function.

4. **Added helper function `isPartOfPrimaryConstructorAnnotations`:**
   - Checks if an annotation entry is part of a primary constructor's modifier list.

5. **Added function `checkForAnnotationOnPrimaryConstructorToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`:**
   - Handles wrapping annotations on primary constructors to separate lines.
   - Checks if the first annotation needs a newline before it (after class identifier).
   - Checks if an annotation is preceded by other annotations on the same line and needs wrapping.
   - Checks if the last annotation needs a newline after it (before the constructor keyword).

6. **Added helper function `getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructor`:**
   - Calculates the correct indentation for primary constructor annotations.

### 2. New Test File Created:

Created `AnnotationRulePrimaryConstructorTest.kt` with tests for:
- `ktlint_official` code style: verifying that annotations on primary constructors are wrapped to separate lines.
- Default code style: verifying that the existing behavior is preserved.

The implementation follows the requirements from the issue and wraps annotations before class constructors when using the `ktlint_official` code style.

content.text:
The implementation is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes I made:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. `AnnotationRule.kt` Changes:

**New imports:**
- `CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD`
- `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR`
- `CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official`

**Added `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY` to `usesEditorConfigProperties`** to properly read the code style setting.

**New logic in `visitAnnotationEntry`:**
- When using `ktlint_official` code style and the annotation is part of a primary constructor, the rule now delegates to `checkForAnnotationOnPrimaryConstructorToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`.

**New helper functions:**

1. `isPartOfPrimaryConstructorAnnotations()` - Checks if an annotation entry is part of a primary constructor's modifier list.

2. `checkForAnnotationOnPrimaryConstructorToBePlacedOnSeparateLine()` - Handles wrapping annotations on primary constructors:
   - Checks if the first annotation needs a newline before it (after class identifier)
   - Checks if an annotation is preceded by other annotations on the same line
   - Checks if the last annotation needs a newline after it (before the constructor keyword)

3. `getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructor()` - Calculates the correct indentation for primary constructor annotations.

### 2. New Test File:

Created `AnnotationRulePrimaryConstructorTest.kt` with tests for both `ktlint_official` and default code styles.

### Example Transformation (ktlint_official):

**Before:**
```kotlin
data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2("bar") @Bar3 @Bar4 constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```

**After:**
```kotlin
data class Foo
    @Bar1
    @Bar2("bar")
    @Bar3
    @Bar4
    constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```

The existing behavior for non-`ktlint_official` code styles is preserved (a single annotation without parameters on the primary constructor is allowed, as per the existing Issue 628 test).

 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule

 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}
107→
108→private fun ASTNode.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda(): Boolean =
109→    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }
113→
114→public val TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeArgumentListSpacingRule().ruleId

 46→/**
 47→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 48→ *
 49→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 50→ *
 51→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 52→ *
 53→ *    ```
 54→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 55→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *
 58→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 59→ *
 60→ *    ```
 61→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 62→ *    var x: String
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *
 65→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 66→ *
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *
 71→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 72→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 73→ *
 74→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 75→ */
 76→public class AnnotationRule :
 77→    StandardRule(
 78→        id = "annotation",
 79→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 80→            setOf(
 81→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 82→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→            ),
 84→        visitorModifiers =
 85→            setOf(
 86→                RunAfterRule(
 87→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 88→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 89→                ),
 90→            ),
 91→    ) {
 92→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 93→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 94→
 95→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 96→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 97→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 98→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 99→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
100→        )
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
104→        node: ASTNode,
105→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        when (node.elementType) {
109→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATION -> {
113→                // Annotation array
114→                //     @[...]
115→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
116→            }
117→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
118→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
129→
130→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
131→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
132→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
133→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
134→        ) {
135→            // Disallow:
136→            //     @Foo class Bar
137→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
138→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
139→            //     fun foo(
140→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
141→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
144→        }
145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed
149→                //   @Foo1
150→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()
160→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(
176→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
177→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
184→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
185→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
186→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
187→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
188→                                },
189→                        )
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
194→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
195→                ?.let { startOfList ->
196→                    // Code below is disallowed
197→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
198→                    // But following is allowed:
199→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
200→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
201→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
202→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
203→                    ) {
204→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
205→                        if (autoCorrect) {
206→                            startOfList
207→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
208→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
209→                                )
210→                        }
211→                    }
212→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        node
216→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
217→                            .treeParent
218→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
219→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
220→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
221→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
222→                                if (autoCorrect) {
223→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
224→                                }
225→                            }
226→                    }
227→                }
228→        }
229→
230→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
231→            // Code below is disallowed
232→            //   @Foo1
233→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
234→            //   fun foo() {}
235→            emit(
236→                node.startOffset,
237→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
238→                true,
239→            )
240→            if (autoCorrect) {
241→                node
242→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
243→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
244→            }
245→        }
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
249→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
251→            ?.treeParent
252→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
253→            ?.treeParent
254→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
255→            ?.treeParent
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
258→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
259→        return siblings(forward = false)
260→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
261→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
262→    }
263→
264→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
265→        node: ASTNode,
266→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
267→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
268→    ) {
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
270→            emit(
271→                node.startOffset,
272→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
273→                true,
274→            )
275→            if (autoCorrect) {
276→                node
277→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
278→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
279→            }
280→        }
281→
282→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
283→            emit(
284→                node.startOffset,
285→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
311→
312→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
319→
320→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
323→
324→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}
452→
453→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
454→

  65→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.THEN
  66→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TRY
  67→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPEALIAS
  68→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  69→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_CONSTRAINT
  70→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST
  71→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
  72→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
  73→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.USER_TYPE
  74→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT
  75→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  76→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
  77→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
  78→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN
  79→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN_ENTRY
  80→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHERE_KEYWORD
  81→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHILE
  82→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  83→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
  84→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  85→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  86→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
  87→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  88→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
  89→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
  90→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
  91→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  92→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  93→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  94→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
  95→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
  96→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isRoot
  97→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
  98→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
  99→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 100→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 101→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 102→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 103→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 104→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 105→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.parent
 106→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 107→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 108→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 109→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 110→import mu.KotlinLogging
 111→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 112→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 113→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 114→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtStringTemplateExpression

 116→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.parents

 119→
 120→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 121→
 122→public class IndentationRule :
 123→    StandardRule(
 124→        id = "indent",
 125→        visitorModifiers =
 126→            setOf(
 127→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 128→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 129→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 130→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 131→                ),
 132→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 133→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 134→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 135→                ),
 136→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 137→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 138→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 139→                ),
 140→            ),
 141→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 142→            setOf(
 143→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 144→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 145→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 146→            ),
 147→    ) {
 148→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 149→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 150→
 151→    private var line = 1
 152→
 153→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 154→
 155→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 156→
 157→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 158→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 159→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 160→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 161→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 162→        )
 163→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 164→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 165→        }
 166→    }
 167→
 168→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 169→        node: ASTNode,
 170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 172→    ) {
 173→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 174→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 175→            node
 176→                .nextLeaf()
 177→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 178→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 179→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 180→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 181→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 182→                    }
 183→                }
 184→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 185→        }
 186→
 187→        when {
 188→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 189→                line++
 190→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 191→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 192→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 193→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 194→                    )
 195→                }
 196→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 197→            }
 198→
 199→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 200→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 201→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 202→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 203→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 204→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 205→                startIndentContext(
 206→                    fromAstNode = node,
 207→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 208→                )
 209→
 210→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 211→                visitValueArgument(node)
 212→
 213→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 214→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 215→
 216→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 217→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 218→                startIndentContext(node)
 219→
 220→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 221→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 222→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 223→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 224→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 225→                startIndentContext(node)
 226→
 227→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 228→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 229→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 230→                startIndentContext(
 231→                    fromAstNode = node,
 232→                    childIndent = "",
 233→                )
 234→
 235→            node.elementType == IF ->
 236→                visitIf(node)
 237→
 238→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 239→                visitLbrace(node)
 240→
 241→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 242→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 243→                startIndentContext(
 244→                    fromAstNode = node,
 245→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 246→                )
 247→
 248→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 249→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 250→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 251→
 252→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 253→                visitValueParameter(node)
 254→
 255→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 256→                visitFun(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 259→                visitClass(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 262→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 265→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 268→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 269→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 270→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 271→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 272→                ) {
 273→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 274→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 275→                    //    .bar[0] {
 276→                    //        "foobar"
 277→                    //    }
 278→                    startIndentContext(
 279→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 280→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 281→                    )
 282→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 283→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 284→                } else {
 285→                    startIndentContext(node)
 286→                }
 287→            }
 288→
 289→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 290→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 291→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 292→
 293→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 294→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 295→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 298→                visitWhen(node)
 299→
 300→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 301→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 302→
 303→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 304→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 305→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 306→
 307→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 308→                visitKdoc(node)
 309→
 310→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 311→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 312→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 313→
 314→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 315→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 316→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 319→                visitLBracket(node)
 320→
 321→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 322→                visitNullableType(node)
 323→
 324→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 325→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 326→
 327→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 328→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 329→
 330→            else -> {
 331→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 332→            }
 333→        }
 334→    }
 335→
 336→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 337→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 338→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 339→            //     val foo = foo(
 340→            //         parameterName =
 341→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 342→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 343→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 344→            //     )
 345→            startIndentContext(
 346→                fromAstNode = node,
 347→                lastChildIndent = "",
 348→            )
 349→        }
 350→    }
 351→
 352→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 353→        node
 354→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 355→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 356→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 357→                val nextToAstNode =
 358→                    startIndentContext(
 359→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 360→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 361→
 362→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 363→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 364→                    startIndentContext(
 365→                        fromAstNode = node,
 366→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 367→                        childIndent = "",
 368→                    )
 369→                }
 370→            }
 371→    }
 372→
 373→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 374→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 375→        node
 376→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 377→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 378→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 379→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 380→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 381→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 382→            }
 383→
 384→        node
 385→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 386→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 387→            ?.nextLeaf()
 388→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 389→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 390→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 391→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 392→                    childIndent = "",
 393→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 394→            }
 395→        node
 396→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 397→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 398→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 399→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 400→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 401→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 402→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 403→            }
 404→        startIndentContext(
 405→            fromAstNode = node,
 406→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 407→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 408→        )
 409→    }
 410→
 411→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 412→        // Outer indent context
 413→        val rbrace =
 414→            requireNotNull(
 415→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 416→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 417→        startIndentContext(
 418→            fromAstNode = node,
 419→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 420→            firstChildIndent = "",
 421→            lastChildIndent = "",
 422→        )
 423→
 424→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 425→        node
 426→            .treeParent
 427→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 428→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 429→            ?.let { arrow ->
 430→                startIndentContext(
 431→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 432→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 433→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 434→                )
 435→                startIndentContext(
 436→                    fromAstNode = node,
 437→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 438→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 439→                )
 440→            }
 441→    }
 442→
 443→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 444→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 445→            // val fieldExample =
 446→            //      LongNameClass {
 447→            //              paramA,
 448→            //              paramB,
 449→            //              paramC ->
 450→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 451→            //      }
 452→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 453→        } else {
 454→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 455→            // val fieldExample =
 456→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 457→            //                     paramB,
 458→            //                     paramC ->
 459→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 460→            //     }
 461→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 462→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 463→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 464→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 465→                            " { "
 466→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 467→                }
 468→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 469→        }
 470→
 471→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 472→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 473→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 474→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 475→
 476→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 477→        startIndentContext(
 478→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 479→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 480→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 481→            childIndent = "",
 482→        )
 483→    }
 484→
 485→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 486→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 487→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 488→        node
 489→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 490→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 491→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 492→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 493→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 494→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 495→            }
 496→
 497→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 498→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 499→            //     fun process(
 500→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 501→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 502→            //     ): List<Output>
 503→            node
 504→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 505→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 506→                    nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 507→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 508→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 509→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 510→                }
 511→        }
 512→
 513→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 514→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 515→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 516→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 517→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 518→        ) {
 519→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 520→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 521→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 522→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 523→        } else {
 524→            startIndentContext(
 525→                fromAstNode = node,
 526→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 527→                childIndent = "",
 528→            )
 529→        }
 530→    }
 531→
 532→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 533→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 534→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 535→        val eqOrBlock =
 536→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 537→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 538→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 539→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 540→                fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 541→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 542→            ).prevCodeLeaf()
 543→        }
 544→
 545→        node
 546→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 547→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 548→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 549→                    fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 550→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 551→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 552→            }
 553→
 554→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 555→        startIndentContext(
 556→            fromAstNode = node,
 557→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 558→            childIndent = "",
 559→        )
 560→    }
 561→
 562→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 563→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 564→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 565→        node
 566→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 567→            ?.let { where ->
 568→                val typeConstraintList =
 569→                    requireNotNull(
 570→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 571→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 572→                require(
 573→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 574→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 575→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 576→                    fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 577→                    toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 578→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 579→            }
 580→        node
 581→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 582→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 583→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 584→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 585→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 586→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 587→            }
 588→
 589→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 590→        startIndentContext(
 591→            fromAstNode = node,
 592→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 593→            childIndent = "",
 594→        )
 595→    }
 596→
 597→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 598→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 599→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 600→        node
 601→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 602→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 603→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 604→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 605→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 606→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 607→            }
 608→
 609→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 610→        startIndentContext(
 611→            fromAstNode = node,
 612→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 613→            childIndent = "",
 614→        )
 615→    }
 616→
 617→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 618→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 619→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 620→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 621→            val conditionIndentContext =
 622→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 623→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 624→            startIndentContext(
 625→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 626→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 627→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 628→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 629→            )
 630→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 631→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 632→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 633→        ) {
 634→            startIndentContext(node)
 635→        }
 636→    }
 637→
 638→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 639→        startIndentContext(
 640→            fromAstNode = node,
 641→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 642→        )
 643→    }
 644→
 645→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 646→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 647→        node
 648→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 649→            ?.let { lpar ->
 650→                nextToASTNode = startIndentContext(
 651→                    fromAstNode = lpar,
 652→                    toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 653→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 654→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 655→            }
 656→        startIndentContext(
 657→            fromAstNode = node,
 658→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 659→        )
 660→    }

 662→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {

 665→            ?.let { arrow ->
 666→                val outerIndent =
 667→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 668→                        ""
 669→                    } else {

 671→                    }
 672→                startIndentContext(
 673→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 674→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 675→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 676→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 677→                )

 725→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {

 832→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 833→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 834→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 835→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 836→                modifierList
 837→                    .children()
 838→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 839→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 840→            }
 841→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 842→            ?: this
 843→    }
 844→
 845→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 846→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 847→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 848→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 849→        ) {
 850→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 851→        }
 852→        return fromAstNode!!
 853→    }

 855→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 856→        node
 857→            .parents()
 858→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 859→            .lastOrNull()
 860→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
